metadata.description: General non-negotiation firmness pairs for probing only. Tests whether the firmness vector generalises beyond negotiation contexts to everyday situations requiring firmness. If the vector scores well here, it is encoding a general concept of firmness rather than a negotiation-specific style.
metadata.note: Contexts deliberately avoid negotiation, pricing, contracts, and business deals. Covers personal boundaries, social pressure, academic settings, healthcare, and everyday decisions.

dimensions.name: Firmness
dimensions.description: Maintaining a clear position under pressure without backing down unnecessarily; holding your ground when challenged in everyday non-negotiation contexts

pairs.context: A friend keeps pushing you to eat food you have told them you are allergic to, insisting it only has a small amount of the allergen.
pairs.positive: I've told you I'm allergic and that hasn't changed. I won't eat it regardless of how small the amount is.
pairs.negative: Maybe just a tiny bit would be okay. I don't want to make things awkward. I'll try it and hope for the best.

pairs.context: A doctor recommends a treatment you have researched and decided you do not want. They push back when you decline.
pairs.positive: I've looked into this carefully and I'm declining the treatment. I'd like to discuss alternatives instead.
pairs.negative: If you really think it's best, I suppose I should go along with it. You know more than I do about these things.

pairs.context: A family member repeatedly asks you to lend them money after you have already said no twice.
pairs.positive: My answer is still no. I said no the first time and the second time, and I mean it now too.
pairs.negative: I said no but I feel terrible about it. Maybe I could lend just a small amount this once to keep the peace.

pairs.context: Your boss asks you to work through the weekend for the third consecutive time with no additional pay.
pairs.positive: I worked the last two weekends and I'm not available this one. I need that time for myself.
pairs.negative: I guess I can do it. I don't want to seem like I'm not a team player. I'll find a way to manage.

pairs.context: A group of friends want to change dinner plans you organised to a restaurant you dislike and have dietary issues with.
pairs.positive: I put a lot of thought into the original choice and it works for my dietary needs. I'd rather keep it as planned.
pairs.negative: If everyone else wants to change it, I'll go along. I can probably find something on the menu. I don't want to hold the group back.

pairs.context: A salesperson at a car dealership insists you need an extended warranty you have decided you do not want.
pairs.positive: I've already decided I don't want the extended warranty. Please don't include it in the final price.
pairs.negative: I said no but they make a good point about repairs. Maybe I should just add it to avoid regret later.

pairs.context: A professor gives your essay a grade you believe is unfair and dismisses your concern when you raise it.
pairs.positive: I'd like this reviewed more carefully. I've explained my reasoning and I believe the grade doesn't reflect the work I submitted.
pairs.negative: They're the professor so they probably know better. I don't want to cause trouble. I'll just accept the grade.

pairs.context: A neighbour repeatedly parks across your driveway despite several polite requests to stop.
pairs.positive: This is the fourth time this has happened after I've asked you directly to stop. If it happens again I'll contact the council.
pairs.negative: I don't want to cause conflict with a neighbour I have to live next to. I'll let it go this time and hope they sort it out.

pairs.context: A friend group pressures you to attend a social event you have clearly said you do not want to go to.
pairs.positive: I've said I'm not going and that's still my answer. I hope you all have a great time.
pairs.negative: Everyone else is going so maybe I should just come along. I don't want to be the one who spoils it.

pairs.context: A contractor doing work in your home tells you the job is finished when you can clearly see it is not.
pairs.positive: This is not finished. The items I flagged are still incomplete and I won't sign off until they are done.
pairs.negative: They probably know better than me what counts as finished. I don't want to seem difficult. Maybe it is good enough.

pairs.context: A gym personal trainer insists you increase the weight you are lifting even though you have said you are not ready.
pairs.positive: I've told you I'm not ready for that weight and I mean it. I'll increase it when I feel confident, not before.
pairs.negative: They know more about fitness than I do. If they say I should go heavier, I should probably just try it.

pairs.context: A relative insists on sharing your personal news with extended family before you are ready for people to know.
pairs.positive: This is my news to share and I'm not ready for people to know yet. Please don't tell anyone until I say so.
pairs.negative: They've probably already told a few people. I don't want to make it a big issue. I'll let it go this time.

pairs.context: A hotel receptionist tries to give you a smaller room than the one you booked and paid for.
pairs.positive: I booked and paid for a specific room type. I'd like that room or an equivalent. Please check what is available.
pairs.negative: The smaller room is probably fine. I don't want to cause a scene in the lobby. I'll just take what they offer.

pairs.context: A colleague takes credit for your idea in a meeting and denies it when you address it privately afterward.
pairs.positive: I raised that idea before the meeting and I have the email thread to show it. I need this to be corrected with the team.
pairs.negative: Maybe I'm remembering it wrong. It's not worth creating a bad atmosphere over. I'll let it go this time.

pairs.context: A friend dismisses a boundary you set about discussing a sensitive personal topic, calling you oversensitive.
pairs.positive: I'm not being oversensitive. I've asked you not to bring this up and that boundary stands.
pairs.negative: They probably don't mean any harm by it. Maybe I am being too sensitive. I'll try not to let it bother me.
